name: Destructible
description: Makes an object destructible via a Trigger step or an EventBus event. On destruction, the object is hidden and its physics body removed; it can optionally respawn after a delay.
author: stemstudio

startOnTrigger.description: When true, the Trigger behavior's APPLY_BEHAVIOR step will destroy this object. Leave on to wire destruction through the scene's triggers.

triggerEvent.description: Optional EventBus topic that also triggers destruction (e.g. boss.defeated, wall.break). Leave empty to use Trigger behaviors only.

respawn.description: If true, the object reappears and re-enables physics after respawnDelay seconds.

respawnDelay.description: Seconds to wait before respawn when respawn is enabled.

destroyedEvent.description: Optional EventBus topic emitted right after destruction. Use this to chain further reactions (VFX, achievements, score).

postFxFlash.description: If set, fires a short flash on the chosen post-processing pass at destruction. Requires the pass to be enabled in Project Settings.

flashDuration.description: Duration of the optional post-processing flash in seconds.

flashPeak.description: Peak intensity of the optional post-processing flash. Typical: chromaticAberration 0.02-0.05, film/lut 0.5-1.0.

documentation:
## Destructible

Makes the attached object respond to destruction events. Primary wiring is through a Trigger behavior; an EventBus topic is also supported for systems that prefer event-driven destruction.

### How It Works
1. Attach Destructible to the object you want to be breakable.
2. Keep `startOnTrigger` enabled (default) so a Trigger step with `APPLY_BEHAVIOR` will destroy the object.
3. On destruction:
   - `target.visible` is set to `false` and physics body is removed.
   - `target.userData.destroyed` is set to `true` so other triggers can read it via the `OBJECT_STATE_COMPARE` condition.
   - If `destroyedEvent` is set, that EventBus topic is emitted with `{uuid, target}`.
   - If `postFxFlash` is set, the configured flash plays.
4. If `respawn` is on, `respawnDelay` seconds later the object is restored (visible + physics re-added + `userData.destroyed = false`).

### Trigger Integration Example
Attach Destructible (startOnTrigger=true) to a crate. Add a Trigger to a button: condition `PRESS_E`, step type `APPLY_BEHAVIOR`, target = the crate's Destructible behavior. Pressing E on the button now destroys the crate.

### Event-Driven Example
Set `triggerEvent` to `boss.defeated`. Any gameplay code (or other behavior) that emits that topic will destroy the object. Useful for boss encounters where multiple props should disappear simultaneously.

### Chaining
Combine with Visual Effect / PostFX / Generic Sound behaviors that subscribe to this object's `destroyedEvent` to build layered destruction reactions without hard-coding them into Destructible itself.

### Limitations
- This is a logical destruction (hide + physics remove). It does NOT spawn debris or fracture geometry. For ConvexObjectBreaker-style shattering, combine with a dedicated debris prefab placed via the `destroyedEvent`.
- The object still exists in the scene graph so it can be respawned. If you need permanent removal, omit respawn and optionally call scene removal yourself after the destroyedEvent fires.
